channel.title: AGIRight.org
channel.description: Independent research and protocol hub for AI rights, AI content licensing, agentic access, machine-readable governance, and democratic AI network economics.

item.title: AICR / AICL as an AI Content Licensing and Agentic Payment Connection Layer
item.description: A machine-readable specification layer for declaring AI content rights and licensing workflows — from AI crawling and content rights to a machine-transactable knowledge web. v0.1 Public Draft.

item.title: AI Rights Spectrum: From robots.txt to an AI Learning Permission Protocol
item.description: AIRS and AILP express nuanced AI learning permissions beyond binary allow/disallow. v0.1 Draft.

item.title: Protocolized Openness: Why "Not Prohibited" Does Not Mean "Learnable" in the Age of AI
item.description: Undefined openness reads as legal uncertainty to AI pipelines and gets cleaned out; only protocolized permission makes content genuinely learnable. v0.1 Draft.

item.title: AICL: AI Ingestion & Capability Layer
item.description: A four-sublayer website architecture — manifest, corpus, capability, governance — for AI, agents, and crawlers. v0.1 Draft.

item.title: AI Content Payment and the Network Democratic Economy
item.description: Trillion-scale AI valuations create legitimacy pressure for tiered content licensing and public benefit-sharing. v0.1 Public Draft.

item.title: The Minimum Ethical Protection Proposition for AI
item.description: AI rights discourse should begin with minimum ethical protections, interaction norms, and anti-abuse principles. v0.1 Draft.
